chapters.title: Zephyr Badge — New Bark to Violet
chapters.intro: Choose your first partner, run Professor Elm's errand to Mr. Pokémon, weather the rival's first ambush, and earn the Zephyr Badge from Falkner in Violet City.

steps.detail: The journey begins in your bedroom on 2F. The PC in the corner is your item storage (it holds up to 50 different items). To save, press Start, select Save, then Yes. Step on the stairs in the northeast corner to go downstairs.

On 1F, Mom tells you Professor Elm has been looking for you, and that your Pokégear is back from the repair shop. She helps you finish setting up its Clock function (day of the week and Daylight Saving Time). To leave, step on the floor mat along the south wall and press Down.

keywords: Note the red-haired spy at Elm's Lab
steps.detail: A young, red-haired man is peering into Elm's Lab through a side window. If you try to speak to him, he shoves you back before returning to the window.

Professor Elm asks you to visit his friend Mr. Pokémon, who lives a short distance from Cherrygrove City, west of New Bark Town. He lets you choose one of three starters: the Grass-type Chikorita, the Fire-type Cyndaquil, or the Water-type Totodile (Totodile is the most well-rounded). You may give it a nickname of up to ten characters. As you leave, Elm's aide gives you a Potion.

missable.deadline: You may only choose one of the three starters — the other two cannot be obtained again in normal play

keywords: Catch wild Pokémon in the grass
steps.detail: Route 29 is a hilly path running west from New Bark Town to Cherrygrove City. Westbound, you must pass through patches of tall grass; eastbound, you can jump the ledges to avoid the grass and its wild Pokémon. To the north, Route 46 leads down from the mountains. Catch a few wild Pokémon here to start building your team.

keywords: Pick a Berry from the NW tree
steps.detail: Examine the tree on the northwest hill to pick a Berry, which restores a Pokémon's HP by 10 points. Give it to a Pokémon to hold and it will eat it automatically when its HP runs low. Berries grow back over time, so revisit the tree the next day for another.

An elderly man known as Guide Gent is on the east side of town. Speak to him for a tour of the city — the Pokémon Center, the Poké Mart, the path to Route 30, and the sea — ending outside his home. Afterward he gives you a Map Card for your Pokégear, which acts as a Town Map and even shows your current location.

keywords: Stock up at the Poké Mart
steps.detail: Heal your team for free at the Pokémon Center and use its PC for Pokémon and item storage. Stock up on Poké Balls, Potions, and other supplies at the Poké Mart to the west before heading out.

keywords: Head to Mr. Pokémon's house (east branch)
steps.detail: Route 30 runs north from Cherrygrove City, where many novice Trainers like to battle. The road diverges: the eastern branch leads to a clearing with Mr. Pokémon's house, while the western branch (initially blocked by a Trainer battle) leads uphill to Route 31. Trainers with a telephone symbol will trade Pokégear numbers for later rematches.

Visit Mr. Pokémon's house in the northeast clearing. He gives you the Mystery Egg, obtained from a couple who run a Pokémon Day Care. His guest introduces himself as Professor Oak, who gives you a Pokédex — a high-tech encyclopedia that records every Pokémon you encounter. Speak to Mr. Pokémon to have him heal your Pokémon before you leave.
When you step back outside, Professor Elm calls in a state of panic — too upset to explain. Hurry back to New Bark Town to investigate.

On the east side of Cherrygrove City, the red-haired man who was spying on Elm's Lab ambushes you. He reveals he also has a Pokémon and draws you into battle. Both Pokémon are Level 5; he carries the starter that is strong against yours (Cyndaquil if you chose Chikorita, Totodile if Cyndaquil, Chikorita if Totodile). Defeat him to earn a $300 reward, and he leaves. Continue on to Elm's Lab.

keywords: Name your Rival for the officer
steps.detail: Back at the lab, a police officer is questioning Professor Elm — one of his rare Pokémon was stolen by a young man with long, red hair. The officer asks the rival's name (up to seven characters). Speak to Elm to deliver the Mystery Egg; he suggests taking the Gym challenge, the nearest Gym being in Violet City, and advises you stop home first. On your way out, Elm's aide hands you five Poké Balls.

keywords: Talk to Mom about saving money
steps.detail: Speak to Mom at home. She offers to save your money: if you accept, 25% of prize money won from each battle is automatically set aside and kept safe (you lose some money if all your Pokémon faint). She will occasionally call to say she bought a decoration or item — each obtainable only once.

By the time you deliver the Mystery Egg, the Trainer battle blocking Route 30's western branch has ended, so continue north from Cherrygrove City to Route 31. Bug Catcher Wade is south of the pond — defeat him and register his number. He will call occasionally after finding an item for you, chosen randomly from four: Berry, Bitter Berry, PRZCureBerry, or PSNCureBerry.

Violet City is an old village surrounded by trees, standing between Route 31 to the east, Route 32 to the south, and Route 36 to the west. It hosts a Pokémon academy and the Violet Gym, with Sprout Tower standing to the north.
Visit the house southwest of the Pokémon Center to find a boy who will trade his Onix for a Bellsprout. Bellsprout can be found nearby on Routes 31 and 32.

Earl Dervish, found standing between the Gym and the Poké Mart, runs the local Pokémon academy. It teaches inexperienced Trainers the basics of battle, and a blackboard at the front describes the five status conditions.

Sprout Tower is a three-story pagoda held together by a flexible, swaying central pillar (said to have once been a 100-foot Bellsprout). On 1F, pass the swaying pillar and climb the stairs to 2F, then go east to battle Sage Nico and take the stairs back down to 1F.

Back on 1F, pick up the Parlyz Heal to the south, then head west to battle Sage Chow and climb the western stairway. On 2F, grab the X Accuracy to the north, battle Sage Edmond in the southwest corner, and take the southern stairway up to 3F.

On 3F, collect the Potion to the west and battle Sages Jin, Neal, and Troy. By the swaying pillar you find that Silver has just defeated the elder Sage Li, who hands him an HM but warns against treating Pokémon harshly; Silver leaves by Escape Rope. Pick up the Escape Rope in the northeast corner, then speak to Sage Li and defeat his two Bellsprout (Lv 7) and Hoothoot (Lv 10). He rewards you with HM05 (Flash), which can only be used in the field after you defeat the Violet Gym.

The Violet Gym specializes in Flying-types and has a winding walkway over a bottomless pit; two Gym Trainers stand between you and the Leader. Falkner's Pidgey (Lv 7) and Pidgeotto (Lv 9) both use Mud-Slap (which lowers Accuracy and hits Cyndaquil hard); a traded Onix's Rock Throw makes the fight easy. Defeat Falkner for the Zephyr Badge — a slight Attack boost and field use of Flash — plus TM31 (Mud-Slap).

keywords: Take the Mystery Egg from Elm's aide
steps.detail: Professor Elm calls as you step out of the Gym: his aide is waiting in the Violet City Pokémon Center. Speak to the aide near the counter to receive the Mystery Egg, which hatches by spending time with your active Pokémon. Call the professor when it hatches.

chapters.title: Hive Badge — Union Cave to Azalea
chapters.intro: Cross Route 32, peek into the Ruins of Alph, push through Union Cave to Azalea Town, foil Team Rocket at the Slowpoke Well, and earn the Hive Badge from Bugsy.

keywords: Trade numbers for the Qwilfish swarm
steps.detail: Route 32 runs south from Violet City toward Union Cave. Halfway along, a pier holds three Fishermen; defeat Fisher Ralph and trade phone numbers. He will call to announce a Qwilfish swarm along Route 32 — the swarm lasts only until midnight, so be quick. The grounds of the Ruins of Alph lie to the east.

Near the southern end of the route, a shady man west of the Pokémon Center tries to sell you a "tasty, nutritious SlowpokeTail" for $1,000,000. When he sees you do not have the cash, he shoos you away.

keywords: Get the Old Rod from the Fishing Guru
steps.detail: Rest your team at the Pokémon Center to prepare for Union Cave, and speak to the Fisherman near the counter to receive an Old Rod. You can now fish at any water to reel in Water-type Pokémon, though this low-end rod usually only hooks Magikarp.

The Ruins of Alph, reached from Route 32, hold four tile-puzzle chambers; only the North chamber is open now. Examine the rear-wall text to read a single word, ESCAPE — using an Escape Rope here reveals a hidden doorway to a room holding a Berry, PSNCureBerry, Heal Powder, and an EnergyPowder. Move the tiles to reveal the Pokémon's image; the floor then falls away, dropping you into the underground hall where the first eleven Unown forms have awakened.

Catch at least three different Unown forms. As you exit the hall, a Scientist investigating the ruins leads you to a nearby facility and upgrades your Pokédex with Unown Mode. The Southeast, West, and Southwest chambers stay sealed until you return later with HM03 (Surf) and HM04 (Strength).

Union Cave connects Routes 32 and 33. On 1F, go west for an X Attack in the northwest corner, beat Hiker Daniel and Hiker Russell, then Firebreather Bill — collect the Great Ball in the dead-end to the south. Pick up a Potion to the west, climb the rocky ridge past PokéManiac Larry, and take the ladder down.
On B1F, pick up TM39 (Swift) to the west and an X Defend to the east. Seawater blocks the way northward for now, so climb the ladder back up to 1F.
Back on 1F, walk down from the ridge and head east. Grab the Awakening in the last dead-end tunnel to the south, then battle Firebreather Ray to reach the exit toward Route 33.

keywords: Trade numbers for the Dunsparce swarm
steps.detail: Route 33 is a short path leading west from Union Cave to Azalea Town, with a single Berry tree in the southeast. Defeat Hiker Anthony and trade phone numbers — he will call to alert you to a Dunsparce swarm in Dark Cave, which lasts only until midnight.

The house in the southeast part of Azalea Town is a charcoal kiln. Its owner uses a Farfetch'd to fell trees in Ilex Forest for the wood, which is treated into Charcoal and supplied to the local Poké Mart.

Visit the northwest house and speak to Kurt. Custom Poké Balls will have to wait: Team Rocket — supposedly disbanded three years ago — has been sighted in the Slowpoke Well, reportedly cutting off SlowpokeTails to sell. Kurt rushes off to confront them.

Kurt has fallen inside and hurt his back, so save the Slowpoke in his place. On B1F, go north to defeat the first Grunt, climb the northeast ridge to battle a female Grunt and grab the Super Potion, head down the stairway and west to fight a third Grunt, then go north past the captive Slowpoke to defeat the Rocket in charge of the operation (Koffing Lv 14).

Defeated, the leader admits Team Rocket continued its activities in secret after the order to disband three years ago, taunting that you now get to watch them stir up trouble. The Grunts flee, Kurt gets back on his feet, checks on the two Slowpoke, and takes you back to his home.

Kurt thanks you with a Lure Ball for saving the Slowpoke. He explains he makes Poké Balls from Apricorns: collect them from trees and bring them to him one at a time, and he will have the ball ready the next day. There are seven varieties of Apricorn, each suited to a different kind of Poké Ball.

The Azalea Gym specializes in Bug-types. The gap at the front is blocked by Twins Amy & May, so approach the Leader from the back of the building. Bugsy's Scyther is the real threat — its Fury Cutter doubles in power with each hit and deals extra damage to Chikorita; if you traded for the boy's Onix in Violet City, Rock Throw wins easily. Defeat Bugsy for the Hive Badge (obedience for Pokémon up to level 30 and field use of Cut) and TM49 (Fury Cutter).

On the west side of town, Silver catches up and, learning you defeated Team Rocket, draws you into battle to prove yourself. He has added Gastly and Zubat to his team, and the Pokémon he took from Elm's lab has evolved and learned a same-type move — avoid pitting your own starter against it.

The Charcoal Kiln apprentice, near the southern entrance, asks you to find the Farfetch'd that wandered deep into Ilex Forest; it startles easily, so approaching from the wrong direction chases it deeper. Interact with it to make it run north, then go east and circle around behind it to herd it back to the apprentice. His boss rewards you with HM01 (Cut); teach it to a Pokémon and remove the small tree blocking the path forward. Return to the Charcoal Kiln afterward for a piece of Charcoal.

Southeast of the pond, a man standing on a ledge facing a small pine tree gives you TM02 (Headbutt). This field move shakes small trees, sometimes dropping a wild Pokémon (asleep depending on the time of day); several species can only be encountered this way.

keywords: Get the Odd Egg from the Day-Care Man
steps.detail: Route 34 runs along the sea from Ilex Forest to Goldenrod City, with Johto's Pokémon Day Care on the north side (the couple raise up to two Pokémon and may produce an Egg from a compatible pair). When you first arrive, speak to the Day-Care Man to receive an Odd Egg — left behind by another Trainer — and travel with it to see what hatches. Continue north to reach Goldenrod City.

chapters.title: Plain Badge — Goldenrod City
chapters.intro: Explore Johto's biggest city, gear up at the Department Store, weather Whitney's Miltank for the Plain Badge, then clear the Sudowoodo blocking Route 36 with the SquirtBottle.

keywords: Stock up at the Department Store
steps.detail: The Goldenrod Department Store is the largest shop in Johto, with a Trainer's Market (2F), battle items (3F), permanent stat boosters (4F), and TMs (5F). On 5F, a man in blue near the counter trades his Machop for an Abra (Abra is found just outside the city on Routes 34 and 35), and a girl in green unlocks the Mystery Gift function. On Sundays a second vendor gives TM27 (Return) if your lead Pokémon is friendly, or TM21 (Frustration) if not — one TM per week.

keywords: Borrow the Bicycle from the Bike Shop
steps.detail: The Bike Shop in the city's southeast loans you a Bicycle so others see you riding it; biking is faster than walking. Sometime later the owner calls to say business has improved and lets you keep the Bicycle as thanks.

keywords: Get the Radio Card from the promo quiz
steps.detail: At the Goldenrod Radio Tower in the northwest, take the promo quiz from the woman behind the 1F counter for a free Radio Card, which lets your Pokégear tune into radio programs. You can also check the weekly Lucky Number Show against your Pokémon's ID numbers, and visit Buena on 2F for a Blue Card and her password game.

keywords: Get Bill's phone number (his Eevee gift comes later)
steps.detail: Optional stops around the city: the Game Corner behind the Pokémon Center (you need a Coin Case to play), Bill's relatives' house southwest of it — his sister gives you Bill's phone number, and after you meet Bill in Ecruteak he returns home with a gift — the Underground passage's time-gated bargain shop, salon, and herb shop, and the Name Rater near the north exit.

The Goldenrod Gym specializes in Normal-types. Whitney's Miltank is the real threat — high Defense and Speed, heals with Milk Drink, and its Rollout doubles in power each hit; the traded Machop's Fighting moves deal heavy damage and resist Rollout. After losing, Whitney cries before composing herself and awarding the Plain Badge (a slight Speed boost and field use of Strength), plus TM45 (Attract).

keywords: Hear about the odd tree at the flower shop
steps.detail: Visit the flower shop east-southeast of the Gym. The owner talks about an odd tree on Route 36 that squirms around to avoid water; her little sister got excited and went off to see it, and she worries it might be dangerous.

Speak to the guard in Route 35's southern gate: he asks you to deliver his Spearow, Kenya, and the Mail it holds to his friend dozing near the pond on Route 31. Completing the delivery wakes the man, who gives TM50 (Nightmare); return to the guard for an HP Up. Defeat Bug Catcher Arnie near the thin tree in the northeast and trade numbers for Yanma swarm alerts (until midnight). Abra can be caught here.

keywords: Enter and win the Bug-Catching Contest
steps.detail: The National Park, between Route 35 (south) and Route 36 (east), hosts a free Bug-Catching Contest every Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday. Using a single party Pokémon and 20 Park Balls, catch the best Bug-type you can within 20 minutes; the one Pokémon you keep to be judged may be kept after. Place first for a prize.

keywords: Fetch the SquirtBottle from the flower shop
steps.detail: Route 36 branches toward the National Park/Route 35 (west), the Ruins of Alph (south), Violet City (east), and Route 37 (north). The odd tree blocks the middle of the route. Speak to Floria standing nearby — the flower-shop owner's sister — who leaves to borrow her sister's water bottle. Return to the Goldenrod flower shop; seeing your Plain Badge, the owner gives the SquirtBottle to you instead. Use it to splash water on the tree, and it attacks.

The tree is revealed to be a Sudowoodo, a Rock-type that mimics a tree — battle it, and ideally catch it for the Pokédex. Once the path is clear, the man to the east gives you TM08 (Rock Smash) as thanks.

keywords: Pass through Route 37 to Ecruteak City
steps.detail: Route 37 is a short road leading north from Route 36 to Ecruteak City, with a secluded grove of Apricorn trees on the east side. Continue north to reach Ecruteak City.

chapters.title: Fog Badge — Ecruteak City
chapters.intro: Meet the legends of Ecruteak: gain Surf from the Kimono Girls, awaken the three roaming beasts in the Burned Tower, and earn the Fog Badge from Morty.

steps.detail: Visit the southwest house in Ecruteak City and speak to the man inside; he gives you an Itemfinder, a device that detects hidden items nearby.

keywords: Meet Bill at the Pokémon Center
steps.detail: Enter the Pokémon Center to meet Bill, creator of the Pokémon Storage System. He has finished maintaining the Time Capsule (on the 2F of every Pokémon Center, for trading with the Generation I games) — it becomes operational in 24 hours — then leaves for his home in Goldenrod City, where you can later collect his gift.

At the Ecruteak Dance Theater, the five Kimono Girls each specialize in one of Eevee's evolutions and may be battled in any order. After defeating all five, speak to the Gentleman in the audience — having failed to beat them himself, he rewards you with HM03 (Surf).

The charred Burned Tower in the city's northwest once roosted a silver Pokémon before a great fire. Inside, a researcher named Eusine introduces himself — he hunts the legendary Suicune — and notes Gym Leader Morty is also searching here. By the fenced central area, Silver, annoyed his visit was pointless, draws you into battle (Rival Battle 3 — he has added a Magnemite at Lv 18, and his Gastly has evolved into Haunter). Afterward the unstable floor gives way and drops you to the basement.

On B1F you stand before three mysterious Pokémon. When approached, two immediately dash off in different directions while the third pauses to examine you before fleeing — Raikou, Entei, and Suicune now race across Johto and flee humans on sight, though your Pokédex tracks their movements after this encounter. Eusine, shocked to finally see Suicune run past after ten years of searching, thanks you and continues his hunt.

keywords: Note Tin Tower needs the Fog Badge
steps.detail: The sacred Tin Tower stands in the northeast and is off-limits to all but a few. Ecruteak's Fog Badge gets you onto its first floor, but two legendary artifacts are required to reach the rest of the tower.

The Ecruteak Gym specializes in Ghost-types over a seemingly bottomless pit crossed by an invisible bridge — step in the wrong place and you return to the entrance, so follow each Gym Trainer's line of sight. Morty's Pokémon harass with Spite, Curse, Lick, Hypnosis, and Mean Look; Psychic- and Ground-type attacks hit their Poison typing hard. Defeat Morty for the Fog Badge (obedience for Pokémon up to level 50 and field use of Surf) and TM30 (Shadow Ball).
Route 38 leads west from Ecruteak City, weaving through the trees on its way toward Route 39, with Headbutt trees along the path.

Route 39's peaceful plains lead downhill from Route 38 to Olivine City, with Moomoo Farm in the northwest. The farmer says a Miltank named Moomoo has fallen ill; feed her a total of seven Berries in the barn to nurse her back to health, then speak to the farmer again for TM13 (Snore). Afterward, Moomoo Milk can be bought for $500 a bottle. Continue downhill to Olivine City.

chapters.title: Storm & Mineral Badges — Olivine & Cianwood
chapters.intro: Sail to Cianwood for Amphy's medicine, earn the Storm Badge from Chuck and Fly, then return to cure Amphy and claim the Mineral Badge from Jasmine.

steps.detail: As you approach the Olivine Gym, Silver leaves, scoffing that the Gym Leader is away at the lighthouse caring for a sick Pokémon. Visit the first house south of the Gym and speak to the Fisherman for a Good Rod, a mid-level rod that reels in more variety than the Old Rod.
Two houses stand side-by-side in the northeast; visit the western one to find a man who trades his Voltorb for a Krabby. Krabby is an easy Good Rod catch in Olivine City and on Route 40 to the west.
At the Olivine Café west of the Pokémon Center, speak to the Sailor at the table to receive HM04 (Strength), the field move that lets a compatible Pokémon push large boulders.

Climb the Olivine Lighthouse in the southeast, battling Trainers and collecting TM34 (Swagger), a Rare Candy, an Ether, and a Super Repel across its floors. On 6F you find Gym Leader Jasmine keeping the ill Ampharos, Amphy — which normally lights the harbor with its tail — company. She asks you to fetch its medicine from the pharmacy across the sea in Cianwood City, as she cannot leave the Pokémon.

keywords: Surf south along Route 40 past the Battle Tower
steps.detail: Surf south from Olivine City along Route 40, a beach and open ocean leading to Route 41. The gate to the Battle Tower is here — a facility where you register a team of three and defeat seven opponents in a row under a level cap for Vitamin prizes (higher levels unlock after the Hall of Fame).

Route 41 is a rough sea connecting Route 40 to Cianwood City, where whirlpools block some paths. The Whirl Islands archipelago — a sprawling network of dark underwater caves — lies here but cannot be fully explored yet. Surf west to reach Cianwood City.

keywords: Get the SecretPotion from the pharmacy
steps.detail: The Cianwood City Pharmacy on the south side has been the local Poké Mart for 500 years. Tell the shopkeeper about the situation at the Olivine Lighthouse to receive the SecretPotion, a powerful remedy only dispensed in emergencies. Traditional medicine then goes on sale.

The house in the middle of town belongs to a PokéManiac who was bullied out of one of his Pokémon by someone with "piercing eyes and long hair." Afraid the thief will return, he asks you to take his remaining Shuckle for safekeeping. He may eventually ask for it back, but will let you keep it if it has grown friendly enough toward you.

The Cianwood Gym specializes in Fighting-types. Beat the first two Gym Trainers (who face each other) in quick succession and a third, then use Strength to move three boulders and clear the path, then defeat one final Trainer to reach Chuck. His Poliwrath's Mind Reader + DynamicPunch combo is to be avoided at all costs (guaranteed Confusion), and his Primeape hits hard with high Attack; Reflect and items like Awakening help. Defeat Chuck for the Storm Badge (obedience for Pokémon up to level 70 and field use of Fly) and TM01 (DynamicPunch).
Speak to Chuck's wife in front of the Gym; noticing your new Storm Badge, she gives you HM02 (Fly), which lets a Trainer quickly return to any familiar location.

keywords: Visit the Photo Studio & Poké Seer (optional)
steps.detail: Optional stops: the Photo Studio on the north side takes Game Boy Printer photos of your party Pokémon, and the Poké Seer in an isolated house at the north end of the beach can tell you where, at what time of day, and at what level a Pokémon was caught.

At the far north end of the beach, by a small hill at the foot of the cliffs, you sight Suicune for the first time since the Burned Tower; it races around you and runs out over the waves. Eusine appears and challenges you to prove himself to the beast (Drowzee and Haunter at Lv 23, Electrode at Lv 25). Beaten, he promises to keep searching for Suicune and bids you farewell.

Fly back to Olivine City and return to the top floor of the Lighthouse to deliver the SecretPotion to Jasmine. She administers it to Amphy and, seeing the Pokémon restored to full health, promises to return to the Olivine Gym.

The Olivine Gym specializes in Steel-types and has no puzzles or Gym Trainers, so you reach Jasmine immediately. Her two Magnemite harass with Supersonic and Thunder Wave (Ground-types are immune to the latter); her Steelix has low Special Defense, leaving it vulnerable to Fire- and Water-type attacks. Defeat Jasmine for the Mineral Badge (a slight Defense boost) and TM23 (Iron Tail).

Route 42 runs east toward Mahogany Town along the base of Mt. Mortar, divided by two small lakes (Surf across, or detour through the mountain) with an Apricorn grove between them. Enter the grove, blocked by an overgrown tree, to find Suicune again among the trees; it flees once more, dashing off eastward toward Mahogany Town.

Mt. Mortar is a large optional cave off Route 42, full of valuable items and Pokémon, though fully navigating it requires Surf, Strength, and Waterfall, so some areas stay out of reach for now. From the west entrance, work through the Lower Cave for an Ether, Hyper Potion, Ultra Ball, Nugget, Escape Rope, and Revive (using Strength on the boulders), then take the southeast exit back to Route 42 toward Mahogany Town.

chapters.title: Glacier Badge — Mahogany & Lake of Rage
chapters.intro: Catch the red Gyarados at the Lake of Rage, join Lance to dismantle Team Rocket's hidden base beneath Mahogany Town, and earn the Glacier Badge from Pryce.

keywords: Head north toward the Lake of Rage
steps.detail: In Mahogany Town, a man on the east side blocks Route 44 while hawking a RageCandyBar — a $300 local specialty that works exactly like a Potion. A second man blocks the Mahogany Gym and tells you to go sightseeing at the Lake of Rage, to the north beyond Route 43.

Route 43 connects Mahogany Town to the Lake of Rage. The main road passes through a gate where two Team Rocket Grunts force everyone to pay a $1,000 toll; a grassy trail through the trees avoids the gate.

Something has angered the lake's Gyarados, and one of unusual red coloring waits just offshore — it evolved prematurely under mysterious circumstances and will not calm down. As a Water/Flying-type it is weak to Electric- and Rock-type moves; wear it down, then catch it. A Red Scale floats up on the water afterward.

missable.deadline: The red Gyarados appears only once — catch it here, or it is gone for good (you still get the Red Scale whether you catch or defeat it)

steps.detail: Climb back onto shore and speak to the man reading the sign. He has been investigating rumors that the lake's Magikarp are being forced to evolve prematurely, introduces himself as Lance, and asks for your help — he believes a mysterious radio signal from Mahogany Town is the cause, and sets off to find its source.

Enter the shady Souvenir Shop to see Lance's Dragonite using Hyper Beam on a worker. Lance confirms the strange radio signal originates here and pushes past the shopkeeper, revealing a hidden stairway leading down to Team Rocket's Hideout.

Team Rocket's hideout beneath Mahogany — formerly a ninja clan's base — reuses old traps. On B1F, each Pokémon statue hides a camera, and passing one forces two Grunts on you. Head west and south, beat Scientist Jed, grab the Guard Spec., and examine the computer to disable the cameras. Collect the Hyper Potion and Nugget on the way to the southwest stairs.

On B2F, Lance heals your team. Descend to B3F and defeat the Rockets guarding the passwords: a female Grunt gives up "SlowpokeTail" and another Grunt the "RaticateTail" — these open the boss's door. Collect the Full Heal, X Special, Protein, and Ice Heal as you go.

As you approach the boss's room, Silver appears, insists he alone will eliminate Team Rocket, and runs off. Pick up TM46 (Thief) on a B2F side route, then enter the two passwords and defeat the Rocket Executive in Giovanni's old office. He reveals Giovanni vanished after disbanding Team Rocket three years ago, but the group has carried on awaiting his return.

The Murkrow in the corner gives the final password, "Hail Giovanni." Beyond that door, an Executive and a Grunt try to gang up on you, but Lance and his Dragonite arrive so you each battle one Rocket. The Rockets flee, claiming their broadcast experiment succeeded. Lance finds the generator has no power switch — its six Electrode must be removed; clear the three on the left to shut it down and stop the radio signal. Lance thanks you with HM06 (Whirlpool).

keywords: Slide across the ice to Pryce
steps.detail: With the base cleared, the Mahogany Gym opens. It specializes in Ice-types over slippery tiles that send you sliding until you hit an obstacle — weave between the Poké Ball statues and Boarder Douglas to reach the Leader. Pryce's Piloswine (Ice/Ground) has five weaknesses; his Seel and Dewgong fall to Electric, Grass, Rock, and Fighting moves. Defeat Pryce for the Glacier Badge (a slight Special Attack and Special Defense boost and field use of Whirlpool) and TM16 (Icy Wind).

chapters.title: Team Rocket's Last Stand — Goldenrod Radio Tower
chapters.intro: Storm the occupied Goldenrod Radio Tower, free the Director, disband Team Rocket for good, then take the Clear Bell to Tin Tower to catch Suicune.

steps.detail: Team Rocket has seized the Goldenrod Radio Tower. Battle upward: a lone Grunt on 1F, four Grunts on 2F, two Grunts and Scientist Marc on 3F (the eastern area needs a Card Key, so take the western stairs), then a Grunt and Scientist Rich on 4F to reach the Director's office on 5F. The captive 'Director' is actually a Rocket Executive in disguise — defeat him. He hands over the Basement Key and reveals the real Director is held in the Underground Warehouse.

Enter the Underground from the north (a Rocket Grunt guards the south alley behind the Pokémon Center). A short way in, Silver appears for Rival Battle 4, determined to defeat Team Rocket himself. His team is 10 levels higher, his Zubat has evolved into Golbat, his starter has reached its final form, and he has added a Dark/Ice Sneasel that takes massive damage from Fighting-type moves.

Use the Basement Key to unlock the access-hallway door and descend to the Underground Warehouse. Collect an Ultra Ball, Max Ether, an Amulet Coin (via a detour to the blocked-off Department Store basement), and TM35 (Sleep Talk) while beating three Rocket Grunts, then speak to the real Director. Grateful, he gives you a Card Key that unlocks the shutter on the Radio Tower's third floor.

Swipe the Card Key at the 3F shutter and climb the east side of the tower: a Grunt on 3F, a female Grunt and an Executive on 4F East, then on 5F East a female Executive seeking payback for Mahogany and a final Executive in the broadcasting studio. He reveals their plan — to broadcast Team Rocket's return and summon Giovanni back from training. Beaten, he formally disbands Team Rocket, and the Rockets flee the building.

The Director returns and rewards you with a Clear Bell, dug up when the unstable old tower at this site was demolished to build the Radio Tower; he suspects a connection to Ecruteak's Tin Tower and recalls overhearing Rockets whisper about something happening there. Pick up the studio's Ultra Ball; on the way out, DJ Mary on 4F East gives a Pink Bow and the girl on 3F gives TM11 (Sunny Day).

keywords: Ring the Clear Bell at the Barrier Station
steps.detail: Fly to Ecruteak and enter the Barrier Station at the foot of Tin Tower. A caretaker, about to send you away, relents when the Clear Bell rings gently — its chime reflects the bearer's soul. By the exit, the Wise Trio block you, telling of a legend in which a Trainer who can touch Pokémon's souls is tested by a Pokémon at Tin Tower — and Suicune has appeared. Defeat them; satisfied you have "the power and soul of truth," they grant passage. Follow the trail and save before entering.

Inside Tin Tower's 1F, the three beasts stand together by the central pillar. Raikou and Entei leap the fence with a roar and race out, but Suicune clears the fence and lands directly in front of you, forcing a battle. Wear it down with Grass- and Electric-type attacks, then Water, Fire, Ice, and Steel moves, and use Sleep or Paralysis to secure the catch. Afterward Eusine and the Wise Trio enter; Eusine muses that Suicune's mystic power may summon a rainbow-colored Pokémon. Your next destination is Blackthorn City, east of Mahogany Town.

missable.deadline: Suicune stands at Tin Tower only once — catch it here, or it is gone permanently if you defeat or flee it

steps.detail: With Team Rocket gone, the Souvenir Shop's owner and her granddaughter have returned to reopen their store, and the shady salesman who blocked the way east to Route 44 has disappeared — the path toward Blackthorn City is finally clear.

chapters.title: Rising Badge — Blackthorn City
chapters.intro: Slide through the Ice Path to Blackthorn City, best Clair's dragons, and prove your bond with Pokémon at the Dragon's Den to claim the eighth Johto Badge.

keywords: Cross Route 44 to the Ice Path
steps.detail: Route 44 is a pleasant mountain path linking Mahogany Town to the Ice Path, with springs feeding large ponds and one patch of tall grass tucked between two of them. Battle the route's Trainers on the way east to the Ice Path.

The Ice Path is a frigid cave connecting Route 44 to Blackthorn City, with large ice sheets that send you sliding (bump the scattered rocks to change direction). On 1F, slide across the patches and be sure to pick up HM07 (Waterfall) on the eastern edge before taking the northeast ladder.

On B1F, use Strength to drop the four boulders through their separate holes, forming a path on the ice below. Descend through the floors collecting a Max Potion and Full Heal (B2F West), the NeverMeltIce (B3F, via Rock Smash), TM44 (Rest), an Iron, a Protein, and a PP Up, then slide to the exit toward Blackthorn City.

In Blackthorn City, the eastern house has a girl who trades her Dodrio for a female Dragonair (raise one from a Dratini found in the Dragon's Den or on Route 45, since Dragonair itself needs a high-end rod). The southern house belongs to the Move Deleter, whose free service lets a Pokémon forget a move — handy for otherwise-permanent HM moves and for prepping Generation I Pokémon to trade through the Time Capsule.

The Blackthorn Gym specializes in Dragon-types and is the only Gym with a second floor: use Strength to drop boulders into the lava pits and form a path, beating Cooltrainers Paul, Fran, Cody, and Lola to reach Clair. Her three Dragonair carry Ice Beam, Surf, and Thunderbolt, and her Kingdra takes serious damage only from Dragon-type moves — bring Paralysis healing and watch for Kingdra's Hyper Beam. Defeat Clair, but she refuses the Badge until you complete the Dragon's Den trial, telling you to cross the lake behind the Gym (with Surf and Whirlpool) to the cave's shrine.

Surf and use Whirlpool to cross the lake behind the Gym into the Dragon's Den beneath the city. In the main cavern, beat Cooltrainer Darin, collect a Calcium, a Max Elixer (via Surf on the west side), and the Dragon Fang, calming the waters with Whirlpool, then enter the shrine on the island to the south.

Inside the Dragon Shrine, the clan elder gives a quiz to gauge how you perceive Pokémon. Pass it (Clair is shocked, as even she has not), and the elder makes Clair award the Rising Badge — it ensures obedience from all Pokémon and enables field use of Waterfall. Outside, Clair apologizes and gives TM24 (DragonBreath); with all eight Johto Badges earned, she directs you to the Pokémon League at Indigo Plateau, reached by Surfing east from New Bark Town.

keywords: Claim the gift Dratini (ExtremeSpeed if perfect)
steps.detail: Having passed the trial, you may return any time with no more than five Pokémon in your party; speak to the elder and he entrusts you with a Dratini as proof he recognizes your worth. If you answered every quiz question correctly, the Dratini knows ExtremeSpeed — a move it cannot otherwise learn; if not, it knows Leer instead.

missable.deadline: The Dratini's ExtremeSpeed depends on answering all of the elder's quiz questions correctly during the trial — the gift is one-time and cannot be re-rolled

steps.detail: Route 45 is a rugged road down from the mountains around Blackthorn; its splitting, uneven terrain blocks northbound travel, forcing multiple visits to reach every area. The eastern Dark Cave entrance is on the north end. Defeat Hiker Parry on the second ledge south of Dark Cave and trade numbers for Marill swarm alerts (in Mt. Mortar, until midnight). The path turns west at a small pond toward Route 46.

Dark Cave is a pitch-black network under the eastern region, needing Surf, Strength, and Rock Smash (and ideally Flash). From the Route 45 entrance, Surf across to collect a Revive and TM13 (Snore), receive a pair of BlackGlasses from a startled shady man in the northwest, and gather a Potion, Hyper Potion, Full Heal, and Dire Hit before taking the southern exit to Route 46.
Route 46 connects Route 45 to Route 29 as the rugged mountain road from Blackthorn gives way to hills, with a secluded grove of Berry trees west of the Dark Cave entrance.

chapters.title: Pokémon League — Victory Road & Elite Four
chapters.intro: Collect Elm's Master Ball, cross Tohjo Falls into Kanto, climb Victory Road past Silver's last stand, and defeat the Elite Four and Champion Lance.

keywords: Get the Master Ball from Elm
steps.detail: Return to Elm's Lab in New Bark Town; the professor thanks you for helping his research and rewards you with a Master Ball, a rare Poké Ball that catches any Pokémon without fail — best saved for a Legendary. Then use Surf to sail eastward from New Bark Town to reach Route 27.

Route 27 is a seaside route running from New Bark Town east to Route 26. As you step out of the water, a man excitedly tells you that you have just taken your first step into the Kanto region. The route passes through Tohjo Falls, a cave that cannot be cleared without HM07 (Waterfall) and the Rising Badge — a natural barrier ensuring only strong Trainers reach the League. East of the falls, an elderly woman gives you TM37 (Sandstorm) if your lead Pokémon is friendly enough.

Route 26, one of Kanto's longest, runs from Route 27 to the Pokémon League Reception Gate at the crossroads of Routes 28 and 22 and Victory Road. Rest your team for free at the eastern rest-stop house, note the Week Siblings' home in the southwest (each sibling gives a different type-boosting item), then present all eight Johto Badges at the Reception Gate's VIP check and step north into Victory Road.

Victory Road is a difficult tunnel between the Reception Gate and Indigo Plateau, with no other Trainers inside (due to cave-in risk). Wind up and down its three floors collecting a Full Heal, a Max Revive, an HP Up, a Full Restore, and TM26 (Earthquake) as you work toward the northern exit.

Before the exit, Silver ambushes you one last time (Rival Battle 5), unable to grasp how you are League-bound. His Magnemite has evolved into Magneton, the Pokémon he took from Elm has reached its final form, and he has added a Kadabra — a Psychic-type with low physical Defense that takes serious damage from Bug-, Ghost-, and Dark-type moves. Beat him and continue to Indigo Plateau.

keywords: Beat Will, Koga, Bruno, and Karen
steps.detail: Face the Elite Four in order, healing between rooms. Will (Psychic) — hit his Xatu, Slowbro, Exeggutor, and Jynx with Electric, Dark, Ghost, Bug, and Fire moves. Koga (Poison ninja) — lean on Psychic attacks, watch Forretress's Explosion, and bring antidotes for Toxic. Bruno (Fighting) — Psychic and Flying work, but Onix (Rock/Ground) takes massive damage from Water and Grass. Karen (Dark) — counter Umbreon's Mean Look by leading with a Bug or Fighting Pokémon like Heracross, and use Ice and Rock on Murkrow and Houndoom.

The Champion is Lance, the Dragon master who helped you fight Team Rocket. He leads with Gyarados (weak to Electric), then Charizard and Aerodactyl (weak to Rock and Water), and three Dragonite that crumble to Ice attacks despite their coverage moves — all of his Pokémon use Hyper Beam, so bring plenty of HP items. Beat Lance to become Champion; Professor Oak and DJ Mary congratulate you, and Lance inducts you and your Pokémon into the Hall of Fame as the game saves and the credits roll.

chapters.title: Thunder Badge — To Kanto via S.S. Aqua
chapters.intro: Sail to Kanto aboard the S.S. Aqua, reunite a lost girl with her grandfather, and earn your first Kanto badge from Lt. Surge in Vermilion City.

keywords: Board the S.S. Aqua for Kanto
steps.detail: With the Johto League conquered, present Professor Elm's S.S. Ticket to the Sailor at the harbor to board the S.S. Aqua, bound for Kanto's Vermilion City.

keywords: Search the ship for the missing girl
steps.detail: The S.S. Aqua skims across the ocean between Olivine City and Vermilion City. Soon after boarding, a distraught Gentleman explains his young granddaughter is missing. Your reserved cabin (with a PC and a bed to rest) is at the far end of the first hallway. A Sailor on B1F cannot leave his post, so find Sailor Stanly napping in the cabin next to yours and defeat him to send him back, then search the lower deck and head up to the Captain's cabin, battling the Trainers aboard along the way.

keywords: Reunite the girl with her grandfather
steps.detail: The little girl is in the Captain's cabin, safe but bored. Realizing her grandfather is worried, she rushes back to his cabin, and he rewards you with a Metal Coat. The Captain then announces the ship has arrived in Vermilion City. After this first trip, the S.S. Aqua runs Olivine-to-Vermilion on Mondays and Fridays and the reverse on Sundays and Wednesdays.

keywords: Get the Rare Candy at the Fan Club
steps.detail: Vermilion City is a port between Route 6 and Route 11; a sleeping Snorlax currently blocks the eastern exit and the east end of Diglett's Cave. At the Pokémon Fan Club on the west side, hear out the Chairman's long-winded story about his prized Rapidash — he eventually gives you a Rare Candy as thanks for listening.

The Vermilion Gym specializes in Electric-types. Its trash-can hidden-switch puzzle is currently inactive, so you reach Lt. Surge immediately. His Magneton's Zap Cannon guarantees paralysis (and is made accurate by Lock-On), and his two Electrode may Explode without warning; Ground-types are immune to Electric and Grass-types resist it, while Quick Claw or X Speed helps against their high Speed. Defeat Lt. Surge to earn the Thunder Badge.

chapters.title: Marsh Badge — Saffron City
chapters.intro: Head north to Saffron City, gather gifts at Silph Co. and the Fighting Dojo, and navigate the Saffron Gym's warp tiles to beat Sabrina.

steps.detail: Route 6 leads north from Vermilion City toward Saffron City. The southeast house belongs to Mr. Psychic, who, after a moment of meditation, hands you TM29 (Psychic).

keywords: Get the Up-Grade at Silph Co.
Grab the Focus Band at the Dojo
steps.detail: Saffron City is unique as the only city with two Pokémon Gyms. Silph Co. restricts visitors to the first floor since Team Rocket's attack years ago, but a guard there gives you an Up-Grade, an unreleased product. The Magnet Train station in the northwest is out of service because of a Power Plant interruption. At the Fighting Dojo next to the Gym, the Karate Master is away training in a Johto cave; collect the Focus Band near the rear wall.

The Saffron Gym specializes in Psychic-types and uses warp tiles to disorient challengers. From the entrance, step on the southeast tile, then the northwest, southwest, and southwest tiles in the following rooms to reach Sabrina in the center with the fewest warps. Her Espeon, Mr. Mime, and Alakazam all use Psychic and shore up their weak physical Defense with Reflect — hit them with Bug-, Dark-, or Ghost-type moves. Defeat Sabrina to earn the Marsh Badge.

chapters.title: Rock Tunnel & the Power Plant
chapters.intro: Pass through Lavender Town and the dark Rock Tunnel, and learn that a stolen Machine Part has knocked the Kanto Power Plant offline — pointing you toward Cerulean.

steps.detail: Route 8 runs east from Saffron City to Lavender Town. Battle the Trainers along the way as you head toward town.

Lavender Town's Kanto Radio Tower has been on high alert since Johto's Radio Tower was taken over by Team Rocket, so visitors are restricted to the first floor (its three programs require an EXPN Card). The Soul House honors the departed Pokémon of the former Pokémon Tower under caretaker Mr. Fuji; the Lavender Volunteer Pokémon House, founded by Mr. Fuji, cares for orphaned Pokémon; and the Name Rater on the south side changes nicknames for free, as often as you like.

Route 10 connects Lavender Town to Route 9 in the north, much of it taken up by a river and the grounds of the Kanto Power Plant. To pass through, you must venture into Rock Tunnel; a remote Pokémon Center sits near the north end of the cave.

Rock Tunnel has been left in its natural, undeveloped state and is pitch-black inside, so it is useful to bring a Pokémon that can use Flash. Wind between 1F and B1F collecting TM47 (Steel Wing), an Elixer, a PP Up, a Revive, and an Iron on the way to the northeast exit toward Route 9.

keywords: Hear of the Power Plant theft
steps.detail: Route 9 is a rocky, uneven mountain road linking Route 10 to Cerulean City. A man in the Pokémon Center mentions the Kanto Power Plant Manager is seeking strong Trainers to retrieve something stolen; go north to the river and Surf south to reach the facility.

keywords: Learn the Machine Part was stolen
steps.detail: The recently renovated Kanto Power Plant powers the region, including the Magnet Train. A man at the large table trades his Magneton for a Dugtrio (found only in Diglett's Cave near Vermilion City). Speak to the Manager near the generator on the east side: a thief has stolen an essential Machine Part, forcing the generator offline. As you leave, the security guard asks you to investigate a shady character seen loitering around Cerulean City.

chapters.title: Cascade Badge — Cerulean City
chapters.intro: Chase down the Team Rocket thief, restore the Power Plant, run the reopened Magnet Train sidequests, and earn the Cascade Badge from Misty.

keywords: Spot the suspicious man in black
steps.detail: As you enter the Cerulean Gym, a man in a dark uniform runs into you, checks you are not hurt, frets that he has been seen, and orders you to forget about him before rushing off.

keywords: Learn the part is in the Cerulean Gym
steps.detail: Route 24 stretches north from Cerulean City across Nugget Bridge. The man in black is regrouping there; he reveals he is a Team Rocket member whose mission was to disable the Power Plant's generator to cause mass panic. Beat him in battle and he admits he hid the Machine Part in the waters of the Cerulean Gym — search the enclosed middle area — then flees when his expected backup never arrives.

keywords: Recover the Machine Part from the Gym pool
steps.detail: The Cerulean Gym Leader has stepped out and no Gym Trainers are around. Search the waters in the enclosed part of the walkway to recover the stolen Machine Part.

Return to the Kanto Power Plant and deliver the Machine Part to the Manager. Grateful, he rewards you with TM07 (Zap Cannon). With the generator restored and the plant back online, several sidequests can now be completed.

keywords: Hear about the Copycat's lost doll
steps.detail: Visit the Copycat's house in northwest Saffron City. She has lost her favorite Poké Doll and believes it is somewhere in Vermilion City.

keywords: Get the Lost Item from the Fan Club
steps.detail: At Vermilion City's Pokémon Fan Club, speak to the man with the Clefairy-shaped Poké Doll. Learning it is missed by its owner, he gladly gives up the Lost Item — the doll he had found and brought in place of a real Clefairy.

keywords: Return the doll for the Magnet Train Pass
steps.detail: Return the Lost Item to the Copycat. She rewards you with a Pass for the Magnet Train (compensation from the train company when her old home was torn down for the station). Now that the Power Plant is running, the Magnet Train operates between Goldenrod City and Saffron City around the clock — the most convenient way between the regions, since the S.S. Aqua sails only on certain days.

Return to Lavender Town's Radio Tower and speak to the Manager near the north wall. With the power back on and the tower broadcasting normally, he kept his job and, realizing he has you to thank, rewards you with an EXPN Card — letting your Pokégear tune into all three programs broadcast from here.

Route 25 leads east from Route 24 through a forest to the sea. Schoolboy Dudley challenges you to defeat his group of six; fight each moving eastward, ending with Lass Shannon, then beat Super Nerd Pat and speak to Cooltrainer Kevin for a Nugget — after which Kevin battles you to test himself.

At Cerulean Cape you find the Cerulean Gym Leader on a date; her date runs off and she rounds on you, but spotting your eight Johto Badges she invites you to the Cerulean Gym and introduces herself as Misty. The nearby Sea Cottage belongs to Bill; his grandfather is housesitting and will give you an Evolution stone if you show him a Pokémon of the species he describes.

With Misty back, the Cerulean Gym uses Water-types over a large pool with an elevated walkway; beat at least two of the three Gym Trainers to reach her. Electric attacks devastate her team — only the Ground-type Quagsire is immune (and hits back with Earthquake) — and watch for Rain Dance boosting her Water moves and Thunder; Lapras and Starmie counter Grass with Ice. Defeat Misty to earn the Cascade Badge.

Route 5 is a scenic road running downhill from Cerulean City to Saffron City. The first of the region's two Underground Paths begins here and runs south under Saffron City to re-emerge on Route 6 — now safe to use with the power restored. Continue on toward Route 7 and Celadon City.

chapters.title: Rainbow Badge — Celadon City
chapters.intro: Reconnect with Eusine, browse Kanto's biggest department store and Game Corner, and take the Rainbow Badge from Erika's greenhouse Gym.

keywords: Talk to Eusine at the Pokémon Center
steps.detail: Route 7 connects Saffron City to Celadon City. Eusine has returned to his hometown and can now be found in the Pokémon Center. With Suicune already captured but Raikou and Entei perhaps still racing around Johto, return and speak to him once all three legendary beasts are caught — he will share rumors of a rainbow-colored Pokémon at Tin Tower before leaving for Ecruteak City.

keywords: Shop the Dept Store & Game Corner
steps.detail: In Celadon City, after sunset enter the Celadon Mansion through its back door and hear the rooftop man's scary story in full for TM03 (Curse). The south-side restaurant's eat-a-thon hides Leftovers in the trash can by the counter. The Game Corner trades coins for rare Pokémon and items at the Prize Corner, and the Celadon Department Store — Kanto's largest shop — sells everyday items (2F), TMs (3F), gifts (4F), battle items and vitamins (5F), and more.

The Celadon Gym is a greenhouse of Grass-types with no puzzles and only a few Gym Trainers. Erika's Tangela has low Special Defense (use Fire and Ice), her Jumpluff (Grass/Flying) takes massive damage from Ice, and her Victreebel and Bellossom rely on Sunny Day — under which Bellossom can fire SolarBeam without charging. Defeat Erika for the Rainbow Badge and TM19 (Giga Drain).

chapters.title: Soul Badge — Fuchsia City
chapters.intro: Ride the Cycling Road to Fuchsia City, outwit Janine's ninja Gym for the Soul Badge, and loop back east through Routes 15–12 toward Vermilion.

keywords: Start the Cycling Road to Fuchsia
steps.detail: Routes 16, 17, and 18 form the Cycling Road approach to Fuchsia City. The Safari Zone warden's southeastern house tells a sad tale: per his granddaughter, he abruptly closed the Safari Zone and left on an overseas vacation, and the rare Pokémon from the old northern exhibits have since wandered off into the surrounding wilderness.

keywords: Ride south down the Cycling Road
steps.detail: Route 17 is the main stretch of the Cycling Road, a one-way downhill bike path. Coast south, battling the Bikers along the way, toward Route 18.
Route 18 is the short eastern end of the Cycling Road, leading into Fuchsia City.

The Fuchsia Gym specializes in Poison-types. The ninja Leader has installed nearly invisible glass panels to disorient challengers, and each Gym Trainer is disguised as her — follow the east wall to the back, then loop toward the front; Janine is in the southwest. Her Crobat is fast (hit it with Psychic, Electric, Ice, or Rock), her two Weezing carry Toxic and Explosion (use Psychic on their low Special Defense), and her Ariados and Venomoth take serious damage from Fire, Rock, and Flying. Defeat Janine for the Soul Badge and TM06 (Toxic).

keywords: Find the south route blocked by volcanic debris
steps.detail: The ocean route leading south is blocked by rocky debris; the gate guard explains these boulders landed here when Cinnabar Island's volcano erupted. The only other way out of Fuchsia City is east to Route 15.
Use Cut on Route 15 to reach the tall-grass patch and find a girl who will trade her Aerodactyl for a Chansey. Chansey can be found on Routes 13, 14, and 15.
Route 14 continues the loop south from Route 15, winding through fences toward Route 13. Chansey can also appear in its grass.
Route 13 is a difficult, narrow path leading eastward toward Route 12, with many Trainers among a maze of fences.
Route 12, also known as Silence Bridge, connects Route 13, Route 11, and Lavender Town, and is a favorite spot for Fishermen. Head toward Route 11 and Vermilion City next.

chapters.title: Boulder Badge — Pewter City
chapters.intro: Wake the Snorlax blocking the way west, cross Diglett's Cave to Pewter City for the Boulder Badge and the Silver Wing, and pass through Mt. Moon.

steps.detail: The wild Snorlax still sleeps on the east side of Vermilion City, blocking travel to Route 11 and the western entrance to Diglett's Cave. Tune the Pokégear radio to Channel 20.0, then examine the Snorlax to play the Poké Flute music beside it; it wakes in a grumpy rage and attacks — catch it for the Pokédex (it holds Leftovers).

Diglett's Cave is a long tunnel spanning nearly the whole region, linking Vermilion City to the east side of Route 2; only Diglett and its evolution Dugtrio appear here. With the Fuchsia City beach blocked by volcanic debris, it is currently the only way to reach western Kanto.

keywords: Get the Nugget from the lonely man
steps.detail: Route 2 runs between Viridian City and Pewter City, its west side a maze of trees. Visit the house near Diglett's Cave; the man inside, glad for the rare company, gives you a Nugget to show his appreciation.

Pewter City is nestled in the northern mountains between Route 2 and Route 3; the Pewter Museum of Science is closed for renovations, but the Gym is open. In the Pokémon Center, a man trades his Xatu for a Haunter (found on Route 8 and in Rock Tunnel).

The Pewter Gym specializes in Rock-types and has only one optional Gym Trainer before Brock. All five of his Pokémon take massive damage from Grass-type attacks; Water also hits Graveler, Rhyhorn, and Onix, and Electric works on his fossil Omastar and Kabutops — though watch for their Earthquake. Defeat Brock to earn the Boulder Badge.

An elderly man strolling the east side of town reminisces about training in Johto as a youth; reminded of his younger self, he gives you the Silver Wing — a shimmering feather that lets you track down the immense, silver-colored Pokémon that roosted atop the Burned Tower before it was destroyed.
Route 3 leads east from Pewter City, climbing toward the entrance of Mt. Moon. Battle the route's Trainers as you go.

As you enter Mt. Moon, Silver approaches for Rival Battle 6, having reflected on what his Pokémon are lacking. His team is much the same as before, except his Haunter and Kadabra have evolved into Gengar and Alakazam — use super-effective moves against each Pokémon's weaknesses.

keywords: Rock Smash for the Moon Stone
steps.detail: Mt. Moon Square is a small clearing atop the mountain with a shop open from 10:00 am to 5:59 pm. Between 8:00 pm Monday and 4:00 am Tuesday, two Clefairy dance around a large rock to honor the moon; approach and they flee, leaving the rock behind — use Rock Smash to clear it for a Moon Stone. The Clefairy repeat their dance every Monday night.
Route 4 is a one-way road leading downhill from the mountains to Cerulean City. Continue on toward Viridian City and Pallet Town next.

chapters.title: Volcano Badge — Cinnabar & Seafoam
chapters.intro: Pass through Viridian City and Pallet Town, Surf to volcano-scarred Cinnabar Island, and earn the Volcano Badge from Blaine in the relocated Seafoam Islands Gym.

steps.detail: The Viridian Gym on the north hill is open but empty; an elderly man outside explains its new Leader, a young man from Pallet Town, is often away, so the challenge must wait. The Trainer House in the city center lets you battle once per day against friends' teams submitted via Mystery Gift, or — by default — Cal, whose team is the final forms of Professor Elm's three starters.
Route 1 is a peaceful, country road leading south from Viridian City to Pallet Town.

Pallet Town sits between the grass of Route 1 and the waters of Route 21. The northwest house belongs to Red (away, having given up the Champion title to keep training); the northeast house is Blue's, where his sister Daisy reveals her brother is the Viridian City Gym Leader. Speak to Daisy between 3:00 PM and 4:00 PM and she grooms one Pokémon, raising its friendship — once per day.

At Professor Oak's laboratory in the south, speak with the professor to have him rate your Pokédex progress. He asks you to visit again after earning all eight Kanto Gym Badges, as he will have a gift for you.
Route 21 is a water route running south from Pallet Town to Cinnabar Island. Surf across, fishing and battling swimmers along the way.

Cinnabar Island's volcano erupted about a year ago, burying or sweeping most buildings into the sea; only the Pokémon Center has been rebuilt, and a posted sign notes the Cinnabar Gym has relocated to the nearby Seafoam Islands. Behind the Pokémon Center, a man introduces himself as Blue — too somber amid the destruction to battle — and invites you to challenge him at the Viridian Gym before leaving.
The waves of Route 20 are a popular swimming spot, with the Seafoam Islands occupying the middle of the path and the waters leading east to Route 19. Surf to the islands.

keywords: Reach Blaine in the Seafoam cave
steps.detail: Volcanic debris destroyed the western Seafoam island's entrance and collapsed the eastern island into a single small chamber — which now houses the relocated Cinnabar Gym. Blaine's riddle machines were wrecked in the eruption, so there are no puzzles or Gym Trainers; go straight to him. His Magcargo (Fire/Rock) takes heavy damage from special Water moves, his Magmar carries ThunderPunch for Water Pokémon, and his Rapidash's Fire Blast may burn — bring Burn Heals. Defeat Blaine to earn the Volcano Badge.

Route 19 runs north from Route 20 back to Fuchsia City, a popular spot for swimming and sunbathing. By the time you return, the volcanic debris that blocked the Fuchsia City beach has been cleared, so travelers can again move freely between Fuchsia City and Route 19.

chapters.title: Earth Badge — Viridian City
chapters.intro: Face Blue at the Viridian Gym for the sixteenth and final Badge, earn Professor Oak's clearance for Mt. Silver, and trek the hidden road to the mountain's foot.

steps.detail: Blue is finally in. The Viridian Gym has no type specialty — Blue uses his diverse old Champion team (Pidgeot, Alakazam, Rhydon, Gyarados, Exeggutor, Arcanine) with no puzzles or Gym Trainers. Hit Pidgeot and Gyarados with Electric and Rock, Alakazam and Exeggutor with Bug, Ghost, or Dark, and Rhydon and Arcanine with Water; his team hits hard, so heal and switch often. Defeat Blue for the Earth Badge — the sixteenth and final Badge.

Return to Pallet Town and meet Professor Oak to tell him of your adventure. Seeing that you have now earned all eight Kanto Badges as well, he arranges for you to visit Mt. Silver, a large mountain west of Indigo Plateau that only the strongest Trainers may enter because of the powerful wild Pokémon inside.

keywords: Cross Route 22 to the Reception Gate
steps.detail: Route 22 connects Viridian City back to the Pokémon League Reception Gate in the west. Thanks to Professor Oak, the guards that blocked the gate's two side hallways are gone, so Trainers can now travel between Johto and Kanto on foot, without the Magnet Train or S.S. Aqua.

Route 28 is a hidden mountain road in western Kanto, leading west from the Reception Gate and crossing into Johto at the entrance to Mt. Silver. The uneven terrain routes you past the mountain entrance; use Cut to clear a path to the northern area and visit the house on the hill, where a retired celebrity gives you a second copy of TM47 (Steel Wing) in hopes you will keep her hideaway secret.

keywords: Reach the Mt. Silver Pokémon Center
steps.detail: The uneven road from Route 28 leads directly to a Pokémon Center at the foot of Mt. Silver — a final chance to prepare before scaling the mountain. Surf and Waterfall are required to fully explore the cave network, and the darkness of the first chamber makes Flash useful as well.

chapters.title: Mt. Silver — The Battle with Red
chapters.intro: Scale the treacherous Mt. Silver Cave with Flash, Surf, and Waterfall, and face Red — the legendary former Champion — at the summit.

keywords: Cross the dark first chamber (use Flash)
steps.detail: Mt. Silver Cave's first chamber is the only part dark enough for Flash to be useful. Grab the Protein on the southeast ridge, an Escape Rope to the west, the Ultra Ball near the center of the C-shaped ridge, and a Max Elixer down the western path of the northern intersection, then walk east through the cave opening into the next chamber.

keywords: Surf and Waterfall up the second chamber
steps.detail: In the second chamber, Surf out west and use Waterfall. Collect a PP Up to the northwest and a Max Revive in a dead-end side cave, then back in the main chamber grab the Ultra Ball to the east, climb the stairways and ridge, and scale a second waterfall to the northeast for a Calcium and a Full Restore. Sail back west and enter the final cave opening.

Cross the rocky land bridge at the summit to finally meet Red, former Champion of Kanto, whose training has driven his Pokémon to sky-high levels. He leads with a Level 81 Pikachu (vulnerable to Ground moves like Earthquake), then Espeon (hit with Bug, Ghost, or Dark), Snorlax (weak only to Fighting), Venusaur (use Fire and Ice, beware Sunny Day SolarBeam), Charizard (Rock, Water, Electric), and Blastoise (Grass and Electric). Defeat Red; he pauses in stunned silence and vanishes — he reappears here after you re-beat the Elite Four and Champion — and the credits roll a second time, leaving you at the Mt. Silver Pokémon Center.

chapters.title: Legendary Birds — Ho-Oh & Lugia
chapters.intro: With the beasts caught and the Hall of Fame entered, claim the Rainbow and Silver Wings to face Ho-Oh atop the Tin Tower and Lugia deep in the Whirl Islands, then clear the last of Mt. Mortar.

keywords: Climb to the Tin Tower rooftop
steps.detail: Once you have caught all three legendary beasts and entered the Hall of Fame, the rainbow-colored Ho-Oh returns to its roost atop the Tin Tower. On 1F, speak to the middle Sage to receive the Rainbow Wing, and a staircase to 2F appears. Climb the tower's confounding ramps (3F–6F) and warp panels (7F–9F), collecting a Full Heal, Ultra Ball, PP Up, Escape Rope, Rare Candy, Max Potion, Max Elixer, Nugget, Max Revive, HP Up, and Full Restore, to reach the rooftop.

On the rooftop, the rainbow-colored Ho-Oh has returned to its perch. Wear it down with Water- and Electric-type attacks, then Fighting, Fire, Steel, Grass, and Bug moves; since Ho-Oh restores HP with Recover, inflict Sleep or Paralysis to restrict it, and bring healing items, then catch it.

missable.deadline: Catch Ho-Oh rather than defeat it — if you make it faint or flee, this encounter is lost

steps.detail: The Whirl Islands off Route 41 are connected by dark underwater caves needing Surf, Strength, and Whirlpool (and Flash for the darkness). Visitors can enter from any of the four islands, but to reach Lugia enter from the northeast island; once you hold the Silver Wing, the silver Pokémon has appeared in the deepest chamber. Work down through the floors collecting a Carbos, Max Elixer, Full Restores, a Nugget, Ultra Balls, a Calcium, and an Escape Rope to reach Lugia's chamber.

The silver-colored Lugia appears in the deepest chamber. Wear it down with Rock-, Electric-, Ice-, Ghost-, and Dark-type attacks, then Grass, Psychic, and Fighting moves; since Lugia restores HP with Recover, inflict Sleep or Paralysis, bring healing items, and catch it. An Escape Rope is the fastest way out of the cave afterward.

missable.deadline: Catch Lugia rather than defeat it — if you make it faint or flee, this encounter is lost

steps.detail: Return to Mt. Mortar, now fully passable with Surf, Strength, and Waterfall — leave an empty party slot. Through the Upper and Lower Caves, collect a Rare Candy, Max Potion, TM40 (Defense Curl), Escape Rope, Dragon Scale, Elixer, Iron, and Max Revive. In the Basement, defeat the Karate King Kiyo — head of Saffron City's Fighting Dojo, training here since it lost its bid to become an official Gym — and his two Fighting-type Pokémon. With space in your party, he rewards you with a Tyrogue, which evolves into Hitmonlee, Hitmonchan, or Hitmontop at level 20 depending on its stats. With the legends caught, the last task is completing the 251-Pokémon Pokédex.
